AgentStreamEvent.description:
Agent-loop stream events. Higher-level than `coco_types::StreamEvent` (which represents raw LLM inference deltas). Adds: - Tool lifecycle states (Queued → Started → Completed) - MCP tool call tracking - Turn-scoped item IDs

Input to `StreamAccumulator`. See `event-system-design.md` Section 1.5.

oneOf.description: Text content delta from assistant response.
Tool use block received from API (input complete). Creates a ThreadItem.
Tool execution has begun (after permission check).

Tool execution completed with result.

`name` is carried here so StreamAccumulator and TUI consumers can reconstruct display state without maintaining their own call_id → name map.
MCP tool call initiated (separate from builtin tools).

AgentStreamEvent.title: AgentStreamEvent

ApprovalDecision.description: TS uses `allow` / `deny` / `ask` for the canUseTool response flow.
ApprovalDecision.title: ApprovalDecision

ApprovalDecision.description: TS uses `allow` / `deny` / `ask` for the canUseTool response flow.

PermissionMode.description:
Permission mode (top-level because it's used by both message and permission modules).

Wire format is camelCase to match TS `PermissionModeSchema` at `coreSchemas.ts:337-347`: `z.enum(['default', 'acceptEdits', 'bypassPermissions', 'plan', 'dontAsk'])`. The serde aliases on the drifting variants accept legacy snake_case input so old session transcripts deserialize cleanly.

PermissionRuleSource.description: Source of a permission rule (ordered by priority: Session is most specific).

PermissionRuleValue.description: Permission rule value — tool_pattern is a glob/wildcard expression. Examples: "Read", "Bash(git *)", "mcp__slack__*", "*"

ApprovalResolveParams.description: Matches TS `SDKControlPermissionRequestSchema` response shape flipped — here the SDK is *resolving* an approval request, so it's sent client→server.

feedback.description: Optional feedback to inject back to the model.

permission_update.description: Optional permission update to persist to rules.

updated_input.description: Optional modified tool input (for pre-approval mutation).

ApprovalResolveParams.title: ApprovalResolveParams

ClientHookCallbackResponseParams.description: Matches TS `SDKHookCallbackRequestSchema` response direction flipped — client→server reply to a prior `hook/callback` ServerRequest.
ClientHookCallbackResponseParams.title: HookCallbackResponseParams

ApprovalDecision.description: TS uses `allow` / `deny` / `ask` for the canUseTool response flow.

ApprovalResolveParams.description: Matches TS `SDKControlPermissionRequestSchema` response shape flipped — here the SDK is *resolving* an approval request, so it's sent client→server.

feedback.description: Optional feedback to inject back to the model.

permission_update.description: Optional permission update to persist to rules.

updated_input.description: Optional modified tool input (for pre-approval mutation).

ElicitationResolveParams.description:
Params for `elicitation/resolve`.

Sent client→server in response to a prior `ServerRequest` that asked the client to collect structured input on behalf of an MCP server (form values, OAuth tokens, etc.). The client populates `values` with the user's input and sets `approved=true`, or sets `approved=false` to reject the elicitation.

TS reference: `SDKControlElicitationRequestSchema` (controlSchemas.ts) — TS uses a single bidirectional message that carries both request and response shapes; coco-rs splits them into a `ServerRequest` for the ask and this params struct for the reply.

approved.description: Whether the user approved the elicitation. If `false`, `values` is ignored and the MCP server sees a rejection.

mcp_server_name.description: Which MCP server the elicitation originated from. Echoed back so the agent can route the resolution to the right connection.

request_id.description: Correlation id matching the `ServerRequest` the agent sent.

values.description: The collected field values keyed by field name. Each value is an opaque JSON payload so typed/untyped fields share the wire format. Empty when `approved=false`.

HookCallbackResponseParams.description: Matches TS `SDKHookCallbackRequestSchema` response direction flipped — client→server reply to a prior `hook/callback` ServerRequest.

InitializeParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKControlInitializeRequestSchema` (controlSchemas.ts:57-71).

Sent once at session start for capability negotiation. Carries hooks, SDK MCP servers, output format, system prompt, and agent definitions so the agent can construct its registries before the first turn.

agents.description: Custom agent definitions keyed by name.

append_system_prompt.description: Text appended to the default system prompt.

hooks.description: Hook callbacks keyed by event type.

prompt_suggestions.description: Enable prompt suggestions in the output stream.

sdk_mcp_servers.description: SDK-provided MCP server names (to skip env-configured ones).

McpRouteMessageResponseParams.description: Matches TS `SDKControlMcpMessageRequestSchema` response direction — client→server reply to a prior `mcp/routeMessage` ServerRequest.

message.description: JSON-RPC message response from the SDK-hosted MCP server.

PermissionMode.description:
Permission mode (top-level because it's used by both message and permission modules).

Wire format is camelCase to match TS `PermissionModeSchema` at `coreSchemas.ts:337-347`: `z.enum(['default', 'acceptEdits', 'bypassPermissions', 'plan', 'dontAsk'])`. The serde aliases on the drifting variants accept legacy snake_case input so old session transcripts deserialize cleanly.

PermissionRuleSource.description: Source of a permission rule (ordered by priority: Session is most specific).

PermissionRuleValue.description: Permission rule value — tool_pattern is a glob/wildcard expression. Examples: "Read", "Bash(git *)", "mcp__slack__*", "*"

ReasoningEffort.description: Reasoning effort level. Ordered from lowest to highest. Provider-agnostic scale — thinking_convert maps to per-provider values.

initial_prompt.description: Optional initial user prompt to run immediately after start.

model.description: None means revert to the default model.

SetPermissionModeParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKControlSetPermissionModeRequestSchema` (controlSchemas.ts:127-134).

TS carries an additional `ultraplan: boolean` field for the CCR web-UI refinement flow. coco-rs intentionally skips Ultraplan (see CLAUDE.md "Plan Mode — Skip Ultraplan (CCR Web UI) Only"), so that field is omitted here — SDK clients targeting coco-rs should not send it.

SetThinkingParams.description: Matches TS `SDKControlSetMaxThinkingTokensRequestSchema` + ThinkingConfig. TS only carries `max_thinking_tokens: number | null`; coco-rs uses the richer `ThinkingLevel` from coco-types which includes effort level and per-provider options.

ThinkingLevel.description:
Unified thinking configuration for all providers.

Replaces both TS EffortLevel and ThinkingConfig: TS EffortLevel ('low'|'medium'|'high'|'max') → ThinkingLevel::low()/medium()/high()/xhigh() TS ThinkingConfig { type: 'enabled', N }     → ThinkingLevel { effort: Medium, budget: Some(N) } TS ThinkingConfig { type: 'disabled' }       → ThinkingLevel::none()

Only 2 typed fields (effort + budget_tokens) are universal across providers. All provider-specific thinking params go through `options` (data-driven passthrough).

budget_tokens.description: Token budget — universal for budget-based providers.

effort.description: Reasoning effort level — universal across all providers.

answer.description: The user's answer to the `AskUserQuestion` prompt.

ClientRequest.description:
Bidirectional control protocol — client-initiated requests.

Each variant carries a unique `method` string used on the wire. The method is the discriminator; params are the variant-specific payload.

See `event-system-design.md` §5.1 for the 22 base variants and §5.4 for the 7 gap additions. 29 total.

oneOf.description:
Resolve a pending MCP elicitation request. Counterpart to the `ServerRequest` the agent sends when an MCP server needs structured user input (form values, OAuth tokens, etc.).

TS: `SDKControlElicitationRequestSchema` — documented as a planned addition in `event-system-design.md` §5.4.
Query MCP server connection status. TS: `SDKControlMcpStatusRequestSchema`
Get context window usage breakdown. TS: `SDKControlGetContextUsageRequestSchema`
Reconnect a specific MCP server. TS: `SDKControlMcpReconnectRequestSchema`
Enable/disable a specific MCP server. TS: `SDKControlMcpToggleRequestSchema`
Reload all plugins from disk. TS: `SDKControlReloadPluginsRequestSchema`
Apply feature flag settings at runtime. TS: `SDKControlApplyFlagSettingsRequestSchema`

ClientRequest.title: ClientRequest

ClientRequestMethod.description:
Wire-method identifier for every `ClientRequest` variant.

Cross-language protocol constant exported to the JSON schema bundle so Python / other SDK codegens obtain the same vocabulary. Consumers should reference `ClientRequestMethod::SessionStart` rather than compare against raw wire strings.
ClientRequestMethod.title: ClientRequestMethod

config.description: Merged effective config as a JSON object.

sources.description: Per-source settings keyed by source name ("user", "project", "local", "flags", "policy").

ContextUsageResult.description: Response to `ClientRequest::ContextUsage`. Matches TS `SDKControlGetContextUsageResponseSchema` (controlSchemas.ts:205-306). Simplified subset — TS includes a rich breakdown grid that's UI-specific.

categories.description: Categorized breakdown (system prompt, tools, history, etc.).

ContextUsageResult.title: ContextUsageResult

RequestId.description: Request identifier. Can be a string or integer per JSON-RPC 2.0. SDK clients typically use integers; coco-rs accepts both.

JsonRpcError.description: Error response payload. Mirrors JSON-RPC 2.0 error structure.
JsonRpcError.title: JsonRpcError

RequestId.description: Request identifier. Can be a string or integer per JSON-RPC 2.0. SDK clients typically use integers; coco-rs accepts both.

JsonRpcMessage.description: Top-level wire message. SDK clients send these over stdin; coco-rs writes these to stdout. Consumers dispatch on the `type` discriminator (NOT `method` — JSON-RPC's `method` field is inside the inner payload).

oneOf.description: Request expecting a response. Correlates via `request_id`.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for correlating the response.

oneOf.description: Fire-and-forget notification (no response expected). `ServerNotification` is the usual payload in coco-rs.

JsonRpcMessage.title: JsonRpcMessage

JsonRpcNotification.description: Fire-and-forget notification. In coco-rs this is the primary outbound format for `ServerNotification` events (no `request_id`).
JsonRpcNotification.title: JsonRpcNotification

RequestId.description: Request identifier. Can be a string or integer per JSON-RPC 2.0. SDK clients typically use integers; coco-rs accepts both.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for correlating the response.

TS emits this when the user runs a local bash command via the REPL `!` prefix (not a tool call). The `content` field is the command output; TS types it as the raw output structure (typically stdout/stderr).

PlanApprovalRequestedParams.description: A teammate's plan-approval request, surfaced to the team lead's TUI for approve/deny. Payload byte-matches TS `PlanApprovalRequestSchema` — see `tools/ExitPlanModeTool/`.

plan_content.description: Plan text rendered from the teammate's plan file.

request_id.description: Correlation id carried back in the response envelope.

PluginInit.description: Plugin init entry (inline struct in TS).

SessionResultParams.description: Matches TS `SDKResultMessageSchema` (coreSchemas.ts:1407-1451). TS has two subtype variants (success/error) unified here with `is_error` flag.

errors.description: Error variant: list of error strings.

num_api_calls.description: coco-rs extension: num_api_calls for observability (not in TS).

permission_denials.description: Permission denials accumulated during the session.

result.description: Success variant: the agent's final result text.

SessionStartedParams.description: Matches TS `SDKSystemMessageSchema` with subtype 'init' (coreSchemas.ts:1457-1494). Sent once at session startup; carries the full bootstrap context the SDK consumer needs to render a UI.

agents.description: Agent type names available for Agent tool spawning.

slash_commands.description: Slash commands available in this session.

version.description: Release version of the coco-rs binary.

oneOf.description: Turn completed, waiting for user input.
Waiting for user action (approval, question, elicitation).

TaskCompletedParams.description: Matches TS `SDKTaskNotificationMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1694-1713). TS calls this `task/notification`; coco-rs uses `task/completed` as the wire method for brevity, but fields match TS exactly.

TaskListStatus.description: Task status wire format — matches TS `TaskStatusSchema` (`utils/tasks.ts:69-74`). **Distinct** from [`crate::TaskStatus`], which is the 6-variant running-task lifecycle enum.

TaskPanelChangedParams.description: Snapshot of the task panel state — tools emit this post-mutation so the TUI can redraw without reaching into `ToolAppState` directly.

TaskProgressParams.description: Matches TS `SDKTaskProgressMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1750-1767). In TS, `description` and `usage` are required; other fields optional. The `workflow_progress` field carries the streaming state of local_workflow tasks — a delta batch of workflow state changes.

TaskStartedParams.description: Matches TS `SDKTaskStartedMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1715-1733). TS has `description` required and `task_type` optional.

ThreadItem.description:
Semantic representation of a conversation thread item. Produced by `StreamAccumulator` from `AgentStreamEvent` sequences. Used in `ServerNotification::ItemStarted / ItemUpdated / ItemCompleted`.

See `event-system-design.md` Section 1.6.

ThreadItemDetails.description:
Tool-specific semantic mapping.

Mapping rules (from `event-system-design.md` Section 6.2): - Bash → `CommandExecution` - Edit/Write → `FileChange` - WebSearch → `WebSearch` - mcp__* → `McpToolCall` - Agent/Task → `Subagent` - all others → `ToolCall` - text content → `AgentMessage` - thinking → `Reasoning` - errors → `Error`

oneOf.description: Bash tool → command execution with output.
Edit/Write tools → file change with diff info.
All other tools (Read, Glob, Grep, etc.).

TodoRecord.description: A TodoWrite item — byte-matches TS `TodoItemSchema` (no `id` field, positional identity).

TokenUsage.description: Per-request token counts (returned by LLM API).

ToolProgressParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKToolProgressMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1648-1659).

Long-running tool progress (Bash, PowerShell). TS throttles emission to ≤1 per 30 seconds per `parent_tool_use_id`. coco-rs StreamAccumulator may emit this independently from `AgentStreamEvent::ToolUseStarted`.

ToolUseSummaryParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKToolUseSummaryMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1769-1777).

Background Haiku-based summary of a batch of tool uses. TS uses this to compress verbose tool output before it's displayed or archived.

ServerNotification.description:
Protocol-level notifications visible to all consumers.

66 variants across 20 categories. See `event-system-design.md` Section 2. Each variant's wire method is generated together with the matching `NotificationMethod` discriminant.

oneOf.description: Session result (final usage, cost, stop reason).
Thread item updated (e.g. tool execution began).

Durable plan-item / V1 todo snapshot — emitted after `TaskCreate`/`TaskUpdate`/`TodoWrite` tools mutate state so the TUI can refresh its panel without pulling the store directly. TS parity: `notifyTasksUpdated` subscriber callback in `utils/tasks.ts`.
Team lead received a plan-approval request from a teammate (via mailbox). The TUI surfaces this as a modal overlay. TS parity: `ExitPlanModeV2Tool.ts:137-141` teammate request flow.
Hook execution progress (TS gap P1 — stdout/stderr streaming).
Output from a user-executed local command (REPL `!` prefix). Matches TS `SDKLocalCommandOutputMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1590-1602).
Files persisted to disk (file upload/snapshot completion). Matches TS `SDKFilesPersistedEvent` (coreSchemas.ts:1672-1692).
MCP elicitation completed (form submission or cancellation). Matches TS `SDKElicitationCompleteMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1779-1792).
Tool use summary from background haiku summarization. Matches TS `SDKToolUseSummaryMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1769-1777).
Tool execution progress (bash/powershell long-running). Matches TS `SDKToolProgressMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1648-1659). Sent at most once per 30 seconds per `parent_tool_use_id`.

ServerNotification.title: ServerNotification

permission_suggestions.description: Suggested permission updates the SDK can present to the user.

request_id.description: Unique correlation id (SDK must echo in `ApprovalResolve`).

HookCallbackParams.description: Invoke an SDK-registered hook callback. Matches TS `SDKHookCallbackRequestSchema` (controlSchemas.ts:366-371).

McpRouteMessageParams.description: Route an MCP JSON-RPC message to an SDK-hosted server.

message.description: The raw JSON-RPC message to forward.

RequestUserInputParams.description: Ask the SDK to request user input (free-form or choice-list).

choices.description: Optional choice list; if present, the SDK should render a picker.

ServerRequest.description:
Bidirectional control protocol — server-initiated requests.

The agent sends these to SDK clients when it needs a decision or input (permission approval, user question, hook callback, MCP routing). The SDK client must reply via the corresponding `ClientRequest` variant.

oneOf.description: Ask the SDK client to approve or deny a tool use. Matches TS `SDKControlPermissionRequestSchema` (controlSchemas.ts:108-121). Expected response: `ClientRequest::ApprovalResolve`.
Ask the user a question via the SDK client (e.g. multiple choice). Expected response: `ClientRequest::UserInputResolve`.
Route an MCP JSON-RPC message to the SDK-hosted MCP server. Matches TS `SDKControlMcpMessageRequestSchema` (controlSchemas.ts:377-381). Expected response: `ClientRequest::McpRouteMessageResponse`.
Invoke an SDK-registered hook callback. Matches TS `SDKHookCallbackRequestSchema` (controlSchemas.ts:366-371). Expected response: `ClientRequest::HookCallbackResponse`.
Notify the SDK that a previously-sent ServerRequest should be cancelled.

ServerRequest.title: ServerRequest

Wire-method identifier for every `ServerRequest` variant.

Cross-language protocol constant exported to the JSON schema bundle so Python / other SDK codegens obtain the same vocabulary. Consumers should reference `ServerRequestMethod::HookCallback` rather than compare against raw wire strings.
ServerRequestMethod.title: ServerRequestMethod

choices.description: Optional choice list; if present, the SDK should render a picker.

ServerRequestUserInputParams.title: RequestUserInputParams

result.description: Success variant: the agent's final result text.

SetThinkingParams.description: Matches TS `SDKControlSetMaxThinkingTokensRequestSchema` + ThinkingConfig. TS only carries `max_thinking_tokens: number | null`; coco-rs uses the richer `ThinkingLevel` from coco-types which includes effort level and per-provider options.
SetThinkingParams.title: SetThinkingParams

TaskCompletedParams.description: Matches TS `SDKTaskNotificationMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1694-1713). TS calls this `task/notification`; coco-rs uses `task/completed` as the wire method for brevity, but fields match TS exactly.
TaskCompletedParams.title: TaskCompletedParams

TaskProgressParams.description: Matches TS `SDKTaskProgressMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1750-1767). In TS, `description` and `usage` are required; other fields optional. The `workflow_progress` field carries the streaming state of local_workflow tasks — a delta batch of workflow state changes.
TaskProgressParams.title: TaskProgressParams

TaskStartedParams.description: Matches TS `SDKTaskStartedMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1715-1733). TS has `description` required and `task_type` optional.
TaskStartedParams.title: TaskStartedParams

ReasoningEffort.description: Reasoning effort level. Ordered from lowest to highest. Provider-agnostic scale — thinking_convert maps to per-provider values.

ThinkingLevel.description:
Unified thinking configuration for all providers.

Replaces both TS EffortLevel and ThinkingConfig: TS EffortLevel ('low'|'medium'|'high'|'max') → ThinkingLevel::low()/medium()/high()/xhigh() TS ThinkingConfig { type: 'enabled', N }     → ThinkingLevel { effort: Medium, budget: Some(N) } TS ThinkingConfig { type: 'disabled' }       → ThinkingLevel::none()

Only 2 typed fields (effort + budget_tokens) are universal across providers. All provider-specific thinking params go through `options` (data-driven passthrough).

budget_tokens.description: Token budget — universal for budget-based providers.

effort.description: Reasoning effort level — universal across all providers.

ThinkingLevel.title: ThinkingLevel

ThreadItemDetails.description:
Tool-specific semantic mapping.

Mapping rules (from `event-system-design.md` Section 6.2): - Bash → `CommandExecution` - Edit/Write → `FileChange` - WebSearch → `WebSearch` - mcp__* → `McpToolCall` - Agent/Task → `Subagent` - all others → `ToolCall` - text content → `AgentMessage` - thinking → `Reasoning` - errors → `Error`

oneOf.description: Bash tool → command execution with output.
Edit/Write tools → file change with diff info.
All other tools (Read, Glob, Grep, etc.).

ThreadItem.description:
Semantic representation of a conversation thread item. Produced by `StreamAccumulator` from `AgentStreamEvent` sequences. Used in `ServerNotification::ItemStarted / ItemUpdated / ItemCompleted`.

See `event-system-design.md` Section 1.6.
ThreadItem.title: ThreadItem

ThreadItemDetails.description:
Tool-specific semantic mapping.

Mapping rules (from `event-system-design.md` Section 6.2): - Bash → `CommandExecution` - Edit/Write → `FileChange` - WebSearch → `WebSearch` - mcp__* → `McpToolCall` - Agent/Task → `Subagent` - all others → `ToolCall` - text content → `AgentMessage` - thinking → `Reasoning` - errors → `Error`

oneOf.description: Bash tool → command execution with output.
Edit/Write tools → file change with diff info.
All other tools (Read, Glob, Grep, etc.).

ThreadItemDetails.title: ThreadItemDetails

TokenUsage.description: Per-request token counts (returned by LLM API).
TokenUsage.title: TokenUsage

ToolProgressParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKToolProgressMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1648-1659).

Long-running tool progress (Bash, PowerShell). TS throttles emission to ≤1 per 30 seconds per `parent_tool_use_id`. coco-rs StreamAccumulator may emit this independently from `AgentStreamEvent::ToolUseStarted`.
ToolProgressParams.title: ToolProgressParams

ToolUseSummaryParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKToolUseSummaryMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1769-1777).

Background Haiku-based summary of a batch of tool uses. TS uses this to compress verbose tool output before it's displayed or archived.
ToolUseSummaryParams.title: ToolUseSummaryParams

TuiOnlyEvent.description:
TUI-exclusive events.

These events are dropped by SDK and App-Server consumers. They drive overlays, toasts, and UI-only state transitions that are not part of the protocol contract.

Per `event-system-design.md` Section 1.7, the design listed this type as owned by `coco-tui`. Since `CoreEvent::Tui(TuiOnlyEvent)` is part of the envelope enum defined in `coco-types`, the type itself must live in `coco-types` to avoid a cyclic dependency. The semantic contract (TUI-only, never sent to SDK) is preserved via consumer dispatch rules in `StreamAccumulator` and `handle_core_event()`.

21 variants (20 from design §4.1 + 1 coco-rs extension).

oneOf.description: Cron job disabled by circuit breaker.

Streaming tool input delta (typing effect).

# Status: reserved scaffolding, not yet wired

The TUI has a handler (`server_notification_handler::handle_tui_only`) that appends the delta to `ToolExecution.streaming_input` for a typing-effect display, but **no producer currently emits this variant** in coco-rs.

The inference layer's `StreamEvent::ToolCallDelta` (a different type, internal to `coco-inference`) is fully accumulated into the complete tool input before the engine emits `AgentStreamEvent::ToolUseQueued` with the finalized input. Consumers see the complete input at once.

Future work to wire this up would require the inference layer to forward the partial JSON fragments alongside the accumulation, and the engine to emit them here as `CoreEvent::Tui(ToolCallDelta { ... })`.

# Why keep it in TuiOnlyEvent (not AgentStreamEvent)

Per `event-system-design.md` §3.3: partial JSON deltas serve a purely UI display purpose (typing effect) and the SDK does not need them — `ToolUseQueued` already contains the complete input. Promoting to `AgentStreamEvent` would burden SDK consumers with partial JSON they must re-assemble, with no behavioral benefit.

Rewind completed — TUI truncates messages and restores input state. coco-rs extension: UI-only because it carries TUI-specific identifiers for message truncation and input repopulation. Out-of-band from the design's `rewind/completed` ServerNotification which carries protocol metadata only.

files_changed.description: Number of files restored (0 if conversation-only).

target_message_id.description: UUID of the target user message. Empty = code-only rewind.

TuiOnlyEvent.title: TuiOnlyEvent

TokenUsage.description: Per-request token counts (returned by LLM API).

PermissionMode.description:
Permission mode (top-level because it's used by both message and permission modules).

Wire format is camelCase to match TS `PermissionModeSchema` at `coreSchemas.ts:337-347`: `z.enum(['default', 'acceptEdits', 'bypassPermissions', 'plan', 'dontAsk'])`. The serde aliases on the drifting variants accept legacy snake_case input so old session transcripts deserialize cleanly.

ReasoningEffort.description: Reasoning effort level. Ordered from lowest to highest. Provider-agnostic scale — thinking_convert maps to per-provider values.

ThinkingLevel.description:
Unified thinking configuration for all providers.

Replaces both TS EffortLevel and ThinkingConfig: TS EffortLevel ('low'|'medium'|'high'|'max') → ThinkingLevel::low()/medium()/high()/xhigh() TS ThinkingConfig { type: 'enabled', N }     → ThinkingLevel { effort: Medium, budget: Some(N) } TS ThinkingConfig { type: 'disabled' }       → ThinkingLevel::none()

Only 2 typed fields (effort + budget_tokens) are universal across providers. All provider-specific thinking params go through `options` (data-driven passthrough).

budget_tokens.description: Token budget — universal for budget-based providers.

effort.description: Reasoning effort level — universal across all providers.

answer.description: The user's answer to the `AskUserQuestion` prompt.

description: JSON Schema bundle for the coco-rs SDK protocol types. Source: coco-rs/common/types. Generated by `cargo run -p coco-types --features schema --example export_schema`.
